results.possibleIdentity: Martin Bock was an electronics engineer active in the late 1960s and early 1970s, specifically associated with the Fairchild Company (Fairchild Semiconductor or Fairchild Camera and Instrument Corp.) in Saddle Brook, New Jersey. During this period, he was a member of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization founded by Billy Klüver and Robert Rauschenberg to facilitate collaborations between the technical and artistic communities. Bock was registered in the E.A.T. Technical Services Index, which matched engineers with artists requiring technical expertise for their projects. His technical background was in electronics engineering, and he later appears to have transitioned to a role at Concept Development, Inc. in Costa Mesa, California, where by the early 1980s he was involved in the design and development of semiconductor memory systems, including add-in memory for DEC VAX and PDP-11 computers.
